convergence, divergence, transform, rift, collision, and subduction state
crust creation, accretion, consumption, and recycling ledger
stable, convergent, divergent, transform, rifting, colliding, subducting, reorganizing, and dormant classifications
snapshotsAndDescriptors: plate, boundary, crust, motion, and regime descriptors
convergence, divergence, transform, rift, collision, and subduction descriptors
crust-budget, activity, reorganization, retirement, and world-feature-intent summaries
dependencies: stable profile, plate, boundary, motion, crust, transition, reorganization, and retirement IDs
explicit relative-motion, thickness, buoyancy-band, pressure, temperature, and event observations
plausibleUses: keep a drifting archipelago connected while its boundary changes from rift to transform motion
harvest newly created crust on a ringworld before an authored subduction cycle consumes it
acceptedKits.experimentProof: A seeded Driftforge route must replay identical motion, crust, regime-transition, reorganization, retirement, and fixed-tick facts into the same plate adjacency, boundary states, crust budgets, activity descriptors, rejections, and digest across archipelago and ringworld profiles.
acceptedKits.idempotencyRule: Motion, crust, transition, reorganization, and retirement IDs commit once, plates and boundaries resolve in stable ID order, and crust quantities are conserved across creation, accretion, consumption, recycling, and explicit losses.
acceptedKits.duplicateAndMergeRisk: Seismic sequences own rupture events, volcanic sequences own eruption state, and Core World owns landform features. Live inventory has no reusable owner for plate adjacency, boundary-regime transitions, relative-motion history, crust creation and recycling budgets, subduction, collision, and rifting.
acceptedKits.promotionPotential: Experimental until multi-boundary junctions, regime and crust-budget replay, conservation, snapshot migration, world-feature handoff, and exploration plus infrastructure consumers pass.

acceptedKits.name: stellar-evolution-domain-kit
acceptedKits.purpose: Advance authored star-like subjects through fuel-reservoir use, core and envelope stages, activity bands, expansion or contraction intents, mass-transfer observations, instability, terminal transitions, and remnant classifications without orbital, nuclear, radiation, or rendering ownership.

coreMapping.nonDuplicatedMeaning: Core owns world identity and renderer-neutral descriptors while orbital domains own maneuver windows and visibility owns observation opportunities; this domain owns authored stellar fuel reservoirs, lifecycle stages, core-envelope activity, instability, terminal transitions, and remnant meaning.

ownership: authored stellar, fuel, stage, activity, transfer, and remnant profiles
star-like subject identity and lifecycle stage
activity, expansion, contraction, and instability history
forming, stable, evolving, expanded, contracted, unstable, shedding, terminal, remnant, and failed classifications
exclusions: nuclear reaction and real stellar physics
snapshotsAndDescriptors: star, fuel, core, envelope, stage, activity, and transfer descriptors
expansion, contraction, instability, shedding, terminal, and remnant descriptors
fuel-balance, lifecycle, visibility-handoff, and render-intent summaries
dependencies: stable profile, star, fuel, transfer, activity, stability, terminal, and remnant IDs
explicit fuel, mass, activity, environment, transfer, and intervention observations
plausibleUses: salvage observatories around a dying lantern star before its last stable stage closes
tend a pocket constellation by redistributing fictional fuel between linked star gardens
acceptedKits.experimentProof: A seeded Last Light Observatory route must replay identical fuel, transfer, activity, stability, terminal, remnant, and fixed-tick facts into the same stages, reservoirs, instability, shedding, outcomes, rejections, and digest across lantern-star and star-garden profiles.
acceptedKits.idempotencyRule: Fuel, transfer, activity, stability, terminal, and remnant IDs commit once, stars resolve in stable order, and authored fuel and transferred mass remain conserved across reservoirs, shedding, remnants, and explicit losses.
acceptedKits.duplicateAndMergeRisk: Orbital transfer owns maneuver opportunities and celestial visibility owns observation windows. Live inventory has no reusable owner for authored star lifecycle stages, fuel reservoirs, core-envelope activity, instability, terminal transitions, and remnant classification.
acceptedKits.promotionPotential: Hold until multi-star transfer, lifecycle and terminal replay, fuel conservation, snapshot migration, visibility and render handoffs, and observatory plus world-simulation consumers pass.

acceptedKits.name: error-correction-code-domain-kit
acceptedKits.purpose: Resolve authored encoded blocks from explicit received-symbol and corruption observations into parity checks, erasures, syndromes, correction attempts, bounded recovery, decoded payload references, confidence, and terminal integrity without owning transport, cryptography, compression, or model output decoding.

coreMapping.nonDuplicatedMeaning: Core Data owns schemas, digests, packages, and migrations while Core Network owns transport; this domain owns authored error-correction code profiles, parity and syndrome state, erasure and corruption observations, deterministic correction attempts, recovery confidence, and block-integrity meaning.

ownership: authored code, block, symbol, parity, and recovery profiles
erasure, corruption, parity-check, and syndrome ledger
decoded payload references and confidence bands
pending, checking, clean, degraded, correctable, corrected, ambiguous, exhausted, and failed classifications
snapshotsAndDescriptors: profile, block, symbol, parity, corruption, and erasure descriptors
syndrome, correction-attempt, recovery-budget, confidence, and decoded-reference descriptors
integrity, ambiguity, exhaustion, failure, and recovery summaries
dependencies: stable profile, block, symbol, corruption, parity, erasure, correction, acceptance, and exhaustion IDs
explicit received-symbol, loss, corruption, ordering, and recovery-budget observations
plausibleUses: recover a ghost relay's navigation blocks from erasures before its correction budget is exhausted
reconstruct a shattered archive map whose parity runes reveal ambiguous symbol swaps
acceptedKits.experimentProof: A seeded Ghost Signal route must replay identical received symbols, corruptions, erasures, parity checks, correction attempts, acceptance, exhaustion, and fixed ticks into the same syndromes, corrected blocks, confidence, failures, rejections, and digest across relay-code and rune-code profiles.
acceptedKits.idempotencyRule: Symbol, corruption, parity, erasure, correction, acceptance, and exhaustion IDs commit once, blocks and symbols resolve in stable index order, and correction attempts never exceed the authored recovery budget.
acceptedKits.duplicateAndMergeRisk: Core Data owns digests and schemas, Core Network owns transport, and model-output decoders parse inference responses. Live inventory has no reusable owner for parity checks, syndrome state, erasures, bounded correction attempts, decoded payload references, and recovery confidence.
acceptedKits.promotionPotential: Experimental until multiple finite-code profiles, erasure and corruption replay, recovery-budget bounds, snapshot migration, transport-observation parity, and communications plus archival consumers pass.

rejectedCandidates.name: signal-triangulation-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: The existing universal game-domain inventory already declares triangulation-kit inside survey/cartography map coverage, and acoustic-signal plus scan/survey families already own adjacent signal observations.

rejectedCandidates.name: investigation-evidence-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: semantic-bounded-domain-kits already installs engine.n.investigation and owns cases, observations, evidence, hypotheses, and conclusions.

rejectedCandidates.name: crafting-transformation-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: survival-crafting-domain already declares crafting-domain-service-kit plus recipe tables, inputs, outputs, stations, and failure rules.

rejectedCandidates.name: shelter-exposure-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: survival-needs and weather domain services already own temperature and exposure descriptors, while generic resource/pressure kits own the reusable scalar transitions.

rejectedCandidates.name: crowd-flow-demand-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: Current NexusEngine already exports promoted OccupantFlowKit and RequestQueueKit operations semantics; a new ProtoKit would be a rename without a distinct higher-level responsibility.

rejectedCandidates.name: formation-assignment-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: The existing Agent Group domain explicitly owns groups, agents, goals, basic movement, and formation state; a slot-assignment-only candidate did not clear the adjacent-domain split gate.

rejectedCandidates.name: infrastructure-restoration-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: semantic-bounded-domain-kits already installs engine.n.restoration and owns assets, diagnosis, plans, work contributions, commissioning, snapshots, and reset.

rejectedCandidates.name: stealth-suspicion-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: sensory-agent-domain-kit and perception-kit already own reusable detection observations, while rpg-social-fact-kit owns suspicion; the candidate added no coherent semantic owner above those boundaries.

rejectedCandidates.name: item-ownership-transfer-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: rpg-social-fact-kit already exposes ownership set, theft, return, crime, and suspicion facts for items; a transfer-only wrapper would duplicate that implemented surface.

rejectedCandidates.name: utility-network-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: A generic utility graph blurred electricity, water, fuel, data, repair, and service semantics. It was split down to the accepted power-distribution boundary with explicit exclusions.

rejectedCandidates.name: incident-priority-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: Core RequestQueue/priority operations plus the accepted rescue-triage severity boundary already cover generic ordering and casualty priority; the candidate supplied no additional incident lifecycle meaning.

rejectedCandidates.name: signal-relay-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: acoustic-signal-domain-kit is implemented and universal-game-domain-kits already declares signal strength, sync, relay, scramble, and beacon-link surfaces; a new relay wrapper would deepen an existing overlap cluster.

rejectedCandidates.name: soil-fertility-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: agriculture-domain-kit already owns farm plot soil preparation, moisture, fertility, watering, growth, and harvest. The accepted replacement is limited to explicit nutrient-pool mass balance across farm or wild land and excludes agriculture operations.

rejectedCandidates.name: atmosphere-weather-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: atmosphere-domain-service-kit already owns renderer-agnostic fog, sky, clouds, wind, precipitation, and visibility descriptors. The accepted habitat-atmosphere boundary instead owns sealed-compartment gas inventories and breathability.

rejectedCandidates.name: flood-water-body-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: water-data-kit already declares water bodies and profiles for ponds, rivers, oceans, floods, and pools. The accepted inundation boundary owns water-volume propagation, barrier overtopping, drainage, and recession rather than another water-body record.

rejectedCandidates.name: heat-pressure-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: Core Simulation and generic-pressure-loop-kit already own heat-shaped scalar resources, rates, thresholds, and transitions. The accepted thermal-energy network adds multi-zone capacity, transfer links, conservation, and equilibrium semantics.

rejectedCandidates.name: orbital-trajectory-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: Core Motion owns trajectories and root-motion frames while Core Physics owns provider frames. The accepted orbital-transfer-window boundary consumes observations and owns only maneuver opportunity, reservation, and arrival-window semantics.

rejectedCandidates.name: water-utility-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: A generic water utility blurred aquifer storage, potable treatment, distribution, fluid physics, contamination, and repair. It was split into aquifer-balance and potable-water-service boundaries with explicit cross-domain facts.

rejectedCandidates.name: radiation-dose-shielding-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: generic-pressure-loop-kit explicitly owns radiation-shaped pressure channels and universal-game-domain-kits already declares radiation-hazard-kit, while the documented radiation-field candidate already names intensity, shielding, and exposure. A new dose wrapper would duplicate those surfaces.

rejectedCandidates.name: avalanche-risk-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: The current ProtoKit idea inventory already defines avalanche-risk-kit with snowpack stress, trigger zones, and runout. Re-accepting the same scope under a domain suffix would not be genuinely new.

rejectedCandidates.name: ecosystem-food-web-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: Accepted ecosystem-population-cycle-domain-kit already owns aggregate feeding, predation, cohort counts, carrying capacity, and trophic-flow descriptors. A food-web wrapper added no separate state owner.

rejectedCandidates.name: manufacturing-workflow-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: semantic-bounded-domain-kits already declares manufacturing and processing under its production boundary, while crafting owners already cover recipes, inputs, outputs, stations, and failure rules. The accepted material-recovery stream is limited to heterogeneous discard classification and recovery dispositions.

rejectedCandidates.name: chain-of-custody-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: The existing idea inventory already declares chain-of-custody-kit and provenance-ledger-kit, and engine.n.investigation owns evidence and case meaning. The accepted specimen-integrity boundary excludes evidentiary custody and owns only physical condition, seals, aliquots, preservation exposure, and analysis readiness.

rejectedCandidates.name: ecosystem-restoration-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: A broad restoration boundary blurred interventions, work plans, habitat fit, population outcomes, succession, and content. Existing engine.n.restoration owns diagnosis and work commissioning; the accepted habitat-succession boundary owns only ecological stage transitions and alternate stable states.

rejectedCandidates.name: immune-response-lifecycle-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: Accepted contagion-transmission-domain-kit already owns subject infection stages, recovery, immunity windows, exposure lineage, and deterministic outbreak state; a second immune lifecycle would split the same subject-state owner.

rejectedCandidates.name: wildlife-migration-route-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: The current idea inventory already declares migration-route-kit for paths and timing and wildlife-population-kit for population groups and migration, while accepted ecosystem population and habitat corridor contracts own adjacent cohort and connectivity meaning.

rejectedCandidates.name: tidal-cycle-window-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: The current idea inventory already declares tidal-cycle-kit with tides and shoreline exposure. Re-accepting the same periodic access and shoreline boundary under a domain suffix would not be genuinely new.

rejectedCandidates.name: landslide-slope-stability-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: The current idea inventory already declares landslide-risk-kit with slope stress, debris paths, and blockage, while terrain erosion and structural support own adjacent surface and support behavior.

rejectedCandidates.name: pressure-vessel-integrity-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: The current inventory already declares pressure-surge-kit, pipe-break-kit, and water-pressure-kit with pressure, valves, rupture risk, leaks, and repair needs; the proposed vessel boundary did not establish a distinct owner beyond those pressure-system semantics.

rejectedCandidates.name: weather-risk-forecast-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: The existing idea and game-domain inventories already declare weather-alert, hazard-forecast, storm-front, risk-forecast, and dependency-forecast owners. A new weather-risk wrapper would deepen the same prediction and alert overlap cluster.

rejectedCandidates.name: reproductive-life-cycle-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: Accepted ecosystem-population-cycle-domain-kit already owns reproduction and births at cohort scale, seasonal-phenology owns biological life-stage timing, and genetic-diversity owns parent contributions and inheritance. The proposed boundary split those owners without a distinct reusable state surface.

rejectedCandidates.name: combustion-process-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: Accepted chemical-reaction-batch-domain-kit already owns reagent reservation, stoichiometric limits, reaction stages, yield, byproducts, and quench, while wildfire-propagation owns spatial fuel ignition and burn spread. A combustion wrapper would blur those two accepted owners.

rejectedCandidates.name: fire-suppression-response-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: The current idea inventory already declares fire-smoke-kit with fuel, spread, and suppression plus hazard-suppression-kit for generic suppression actions and costs; accepted wildfire propagation also owns containment and suppression facts.

rejectedCandidates.name: debt-obligation-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: The current idea inventory already declares invoice-settlement-kit, debt-pressure-kit, social-debt-kit, promise-contract-kit, and contract-ledger-kit, while accepted negotiation commitments own fulfillment, breach, and settlement.

rejectedCandidates.name: soil-moisture-balance-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: The current idea inventory explicitly declares soil-moisture-kit, and agriculture-domain-kit already owns plot watering and fertility observations. Watershed runoff was accepted instead because it owns distinct catchment partition, routing, storage, and outlet hydrograph semantics.

rejectedCandidates.name: collective-consensus-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: Accepted governance-resolution-domain-kit already owns proposals, quorum, ballots, ratification, enactment, challenge, and appeal, while collective resolve owns group rally and break state. A consensus wrapper did not establish a separate lifecycle beyond those owners.

rejectedCandidates.name: sediment-transport-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: The current ProtoKit idea inventory already declares sediment-flow-kit for sediment movement and buildup, while terrain-erosion-solver-domain-kit already emits sediment and deposition deltas. A second transport boundary did not establish a distinct owner beyond those existing surfaces.

rejectedCandidates.name: market-price-discovery-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: The current inventory already declares economy-domain-kit plus market-pressure, price-band, procurement-market, economic-shock, market-forecast, price-mutation, and trade-resolution owners. A new price-discovery wrapper would deepen the same supply, demand, price, and trade cluster.

rejectedCandidates.name: geothermal-reservoir-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: The proposed reservoir scope blurred accepted aquifer storage, thermal transfer, volcanic state, power distribution, pressure observations, extraction infrastructure, and authored geology. It belongs as an experiment composition until one narrower reusable owner emerges.

rejectedCandidates.name: organism-metabolism-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: The proposal mixed individual survival resources, feeding, chemical conversion, respiration, growth, waste, population outcomes, and creature physiology without a stable atomic owner. Existing resource, ecosystem, carbon, oxygen, and decomposition boundaries already own the adjacent state.

rejectedCandidates.name: dialect-evolution-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: The proposal blurred accepted language comprehension, rumor lineage, cultural norm adoption, group identity, authored lexicons, and dialogue content. It did not isolate a deterministic responsibility that could be proven without recreating those owners.

rejectedCandidates.name: osmosis-balance-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: Accepted selective-membrane-transport-domain-kit already owns supplied gradients, permeability, selectivity, species flux, retention, fouling, integrity, breakthrough, and regeneration. A separate osmosis wrapper would split the same membrane transport state.

rejectedCandidates.name: catalyst-activity-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: Accepted chemical-reaction-batch-domain-kit already owns catalyst batch references, catalyst-effect observations, reaction progress, yield, byproducts, quench, and terminal outcomes. A second catalyst lifecycle would split that accepted reaction owner.

rejectedCandidates.name: brittle-fracture-propagation-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: Accepted material-fatigue-domain-kit already owns crack initiation and growth while structural-support-network-domain-kit owns ordered support failure propagation. The proposed fracture wrapper did not isolate a third durable state owner beyond those boundaries.

rejectedCandidates.name: triboelectric-charging-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: Accepted mechanical-wear-domain-kit owns contact-pair and debris history while dielectric-interaction-domain-kit owns polarization, stored charge, coupling, discharge, and breakdown risk. Triboelectric behavior is a composition fact between those owners, not another charge ledger.

rejectedCandidates.name: permafrost-thaw-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: Accepted cryosphere-mass-balance, material-phase-transition, thermal-energy-network, and carbon-cycle contracts already own frozen mass, phase fractions, heat transfer, and carbon release. The proposed permafrost scope was a site-specific composition of those owners.

rejectedCandidates.name: ocean-carbonate-balance-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: Accepted aqueous-acid-base, biogeochemical-carbon, water-salinity, and aquatic-oxygen contracts already own alkalinity, carbon pools, salt inventories, and oxygen demand. A carbonate wrapper would blur their explicit cross-domain composition.

rejectedCandidates.name: biological-tissue-repair-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: Existing damage-health kits already own health, wounds, healing, restoration, defeat, and invulnerability, while status-effect and contagion owners cover staged subject conditions. The proposed repair lifecycle did not clear that existing healing boundary.

rejectedCandidates.name: powder-compaction-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: Accepted granular-material-state-domain-kit already owns packing and compaction while plastic-deformation-domain-kit owns load-driven yield and permanent set. The accepted sintering boundary starts after a compact exists and owns only neck formation, pore closure, densification, and fired quality.

rejectedCandidates.name: organism-thermoregulation-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: The existing survival-needs-domain-service-kit explicitly owns health, hunger, thirst, stamina, temperature, sleep, and exposure descriptors, while Core and generic pressure meters own threshold resources. A second organism-temperature ledger did not clear that implemented semantic boundary.

rejectedCandidates.name: refrigeration-cycle-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: Accepted thermal-energy-network-domain-kit owns zone heat transfer and material-phase-transition-domain-kit owns latent phase changes, while power distribution owns supplied service. A refrigeration cycle is a composition of those owners plus host-authored machinery observations.

rejectedCandidates.name: dune-migration-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: The current idea inventory already declares sediment-flow-kit, terrain-erosion-solver-domain-kit emits sediment and deposition deltas, and wind fields provide transport observations. A dune wrapper would specialize the same sediment and terrain state to one landform.

rejectedCandidates.name: electroplating-deposition-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: Accepted chemical-reaction batches own conversion and yield, crystal nucleation owns growth fronts and defects, corrosion owns surface loss, and power distribution owns electrical service. Electroplating did not isolate durable state beyond that explicit composition.

rejectedCandidates.name: cloud-microphysics-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: Existing atmosphere and cloud families already own cloud, humidity, precipitation, and visibility descriptors, while accepted bulk phase, capillary, and aerosol boundaries own adjacent condensation and suspended-particle state. The proposal blurred weather generation with those owners.

rejectedCandidates.name: thermoelectric-transduction-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: Accepted thermal-energy-network, semiconductor-carrier, and power-distribution contracts already own heat transfer, carrier populations, and electrical service. A thermoelectric wrapper did not isolate durable state beyond their explicit adapter-backed composition.

rejectedCandidates.name: metamorphic-rock-transformation-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: Accepted material-phase-transition, crystal-nucleation-growth, plastic-deformation, and thermal-energy contracts already own phase, crystal, strain, and heat state. The proposed rock boundary was an authored geology composition rather than a separate reusable owner.

rejectedCandidates.name: neural-plasticity-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: Accepted behavioral-conditioning-domain-kit already owns cue-action-outcome acquisition, reinforcement, extinction, generalization, and discrimination, while Core Agent owns observations and decision cycles. Neural naming did not establish a distinct reusable state surface.

rejectedCandidates.name: soil-erosion-profile-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: The implemented terrain-erosion-solver-domain-kit already emits erosion, weathering, roughness, sediment, and deposition deltas, while the documented sediment-flow boundary owns movement. Soil profile development was accepted only after excluding erosion and sediment ownership.

rejectedCandidates.name: planetary-climate-cycle-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: The proposal blurred existing atmosphere and weather descriptors with accepted thermal, carbon, cryosphere, aerosol, salinity, ocean-oxygen, and phenology owners. It remains an experiment composition until a narrower state owner emerges.

rejectedCandidates.name: digital-logic-circuit-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: Accepted semiconductor-carrier and power-distribution contracts already own carrier and service state, while Core Simulation owns deterministic resolution. The proposed circuit graph bundled device composition, authored puzzle logic, and electrical solving without a distinct durable owner.
